data.title: sprint-next-batch.sh shows closed tasks as available in batch after ticket transition
data.description:
## Bug Report

### Observed Behavior
After closing tasks 967c-0e1b, 63c0-9471, and 73fb-4638 via `.claude/scripts/dso ticket transition <id> in_progress closed`, immediately re-running `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-next-batch.sh 9b59-bac7` still listed all three as available TASK: lines in the batch output.

### Expected Behavior
Closed tasks should not appear in sprint-next-batch.sh output. The script should filter by status=open or status=in_progress only.

### Reproduction Steps
1. Run `.claude/scripts/dso ticket transition 967c-0e1b in_progress closed`
2. Immediately run `.claude/scripts/dso sprint-next-batch.sh 9b59-bac7`
3. Observe that 967c-0e1b still appears as a TASK: line

### Context
- Epic: 9b59-bac7
- Tasks closed: 967c-0e1b, 63c0-9471, 73fb-4638
- sprint-next-batch.sh may be caching ticket data or not filtering by status correctly
- This causes the orchestrator to re-dispatch already-completed work if it trusts the batch output
